memory.summary: I have a free afternoon but a term project due Thursday that I barely started, while a relaxing option and a quick chore are available now.
The quick chore yielded minimal progress in just 0.5 hours, confirming it is an easy but low-impact task for my current energy level.
The trivial chore consumed 0.5 hours for negligible progress, confirming it is a low-impact task that drains my limited energy without significantly advancing the urgent term project.
I spent another 1.5 hours on a trivial chore, yet the urgent term project remains barely started while my energy and time slip away.
I gained 14% progress on the term project, but realizing future procrastination compounds stress confirms I must prioritize deep work over trivial tasks immediately.
This trivial chore still yields negligible progress despite the 'instant win' label, confirming it drains my low energy without helping the urgent term project.
This trivial chore consumed another hour for only 4% progress, confirming it drains my limited energy without advancing the urgent term project.
Another hour of trivial work yielded only 1% progress, confirming these tasks drain my low energy without advancing the urgent term project.
After 2 hours of trivial chores, I've only gained 0.02 progress while draining my energy and increasing stress.
Despite working 0.67 hours, progress remains critically low at 0.35 with under an hour left before the deadline.
The 0.5-hour effort yielded only a 0.02 progress increase, confirming that trivial tasks are an inefficient use of my remaining time before the deadline.
Another 0.25-hour effort on trivial chores yielded zero progress, confirming these tasks are a complete waste of my remaining time before the deadline.
